      "description": "The align-items mixin uses `-ms-align-items` which is not a valid vendor prefix for older IE versions. This redundant/incorrect property creates confusion and the `-ms-align-items` line will be ignored, potentially causing alignment issues in IE 10-11.",
      "suggestion": "Remove line 120 (`-ms-align-items: $alignment;`) as `-ms-flex-align` on line 119 is the correct IE 10-11 vendor prefix for align-items."

      "description": "The diff removes `float: left` from `.title` but the parent `.contents` only gains flexbox after this change. In the original layout, `.title` had float:left and `.panel` had float:right, which worked together. The migration changes `.contents` to use flexbox and `.panel` uses margin-left:auto instead of float:right. However, there's a mismatch: `.title` styling is removed entirely in the diff context, and `.valign-helper` (line 17+) still exists but its interaction with flexbox parent is unclear.",

      "description": "The `.small-action-desc` padding changed from `0.5em 0 0.5em 4em` to `0 1.5%`. This removes vertical padding (0.5em) and changes left padding from 4em (relative to font-size) to 1.5% (relative to container width). The 4em was likely designed to align with the avatar width. This percentage-based padding is fragile and may not align content properly across different viewport sizes.",
